output:
Reviewing story `27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer:quality` now. I’m locating the implementation and diff first, then I’ll verify it against correctness, security, architecture conventions, and the available acceptance context.I found the story doc and the implementation lives in `workflow-runner`, `workflow-visualizer`, related types, and new tests. Next I’m reading the story requirements and the actual diff side by side so I can check AC coverage instead of only test status.The diff shows this story was expanded into a broader parser/runner refactor. I’m drilling into the actual runner and parser code now because the main risk is whether the inspect wiring still reflects the root run-machine snapshot, not child actors or inferred state.The targeted tests passed, and the build got through codegen/startup cleanly. I’m still checking the remaining failure modes manually because this review is about whether the implementation matches the story contract, not just whether it compiles.I’ve isolated the main risk: the runner now intentionally processes every `@xstate.snapshot`, not just the root run actor. That matters because several child machine snapshots do not carry enough context for the visualizer and will produce misleading rows, so I’m validating that against the actual machine shapes before I write the verdict.<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. High: the inspect wiring no longer limits visualization updates to the root run actor. Child actors are spawned at [workflow-run-machine.ts:77](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-run-machine.ts:77), [workflow-epic-machine.ts:122](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts:122), and [workflow-epic-machine.ts:167](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-epic-machine.ts:167), and XState inspect emits their snapshots too. The callback in [workflow-runner.ts:161](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts:161) processes every `@xstate.snapshot` without checking `actorRef`, then emits `workflow-viz` at [workflow-runner.ts:172](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts:172). That violates the story contract that the run-machine/root actor drives visualization [27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:10](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:10) [27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:52](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:52), and it can render incorrect rows because `snapshotToPosition()` falls back to generic flow parsing for snapshots that do not carry run-level context [workflow-visualizer.ts:235](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-visualizer.ts:235).

2. Medium: the new tests encode the wrong requirement, so AC coverage is not trustworthy. [workflow-inspect.test.ts:392](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-inspect.test.ts:392) explicitly asserts that all snapshot events should be processed “regardless of actorRef”, which is the opposite of AC #2/#4/#5 and AD5 in the story spec [27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:68](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:68) [27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:71](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/27-3-wire-inspect-api-to-visualizer.md:71). As written, the quality suite will pass while the implementation violates the architecture boundary it was supposed to verify.
</issues>

<metrics tests-passed="153" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="2" />

`npm run build`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run test:unit -- workflow-inspect workflow-visualizer workflow-runner` all passed. I did not find a separate security-specific issue beyond the correctness/architecture problems above.
